items.title: The Word Became Flesh and Picked Up a Hammer
items.summary: Theological reflection on the Incarnation and its implications for understanding work, craftsmanship, and human dignity through manual labor.
items.ggiConnection: Strongly aligns with GGI's integration of faith and culture, connecting incarnational theology to practical life and human dignity.

items.title: Ratzinger in the Whirlwind
items.summary: First Things explores Benedict XVI/Joseph Ratzinger's theological contributions during turbulent periods in Church history. High intellectual depth with strong relevance to Catholic intellectual tradition.
items.ggiConnection: Ratzinger's emphasis on faith and reason strongly aligns with GGI's foundational approach to Catholic intellectual life

items.title: Declinism Is Easy: Art, Beauty, and the Need to Make it New
items.summary: Notre Dame's Church Life Journal critiques cultural pessimism while advocating for authentic artistic renewal rooted in beauty and tradition. Strong alignment with Catholic aesthetics and cultural renewal.
items.ggiConnection: Directly connects to GGI's emphasis on beauty, cultural renewal, and the importance of authentic Catholic artistic expression

items.title: Poetry as Gift: Eucharistic Miracle
items.summary: Church Life Journal explores the intersection of poetry, sacramental theology, and the Eucharist as gift and miracle. Deep theological reflection on liturgy and literary expression.
items.ggiConnection: Aligns perfectly with GGI's focus on liturgy, beauty, and the integration of faith with literary culture

items.title: Seeing Woman through the Eyes of Gertrud von le Fort
items.summary: Intellectual analysis of Catholic writer Gertrud von le Fort's theological anthropology and understanding of womanhood in Catholic thought.
items.ggiConnection: Excellent fit for GGI's focus on Catholic intellectual tradition, Great Books authors, and substantive theological reflection

items.title: Forming Families to Sing With Angels
items.summary: Explores the role of liturgical music in family formation, examining how sacred song shapes domestic spirituality and intergenerational faith transmission.
items.ggiConnection: Directly aligns with GGI themes of liturgy, beauty, and family formation in Catholic cultural renewal.

items.title: Francis X. Weiser, SJ, the Domestic Heortologist, Part One: Biography
items.summary: Biographical study of Jesuit scholar Francis X. Weiser, known for his expertise in liturgical seasons and domestic religious observances.
items.ggiConnection: Aligns with GGI's scholarly approach to liturgical tradition and domestic spiritual formation.

items.title: Against Concelebration: The Remarkable Intervention of Archbishop Paul-Pierre Philippe OP at Vatican II
items.summary: Historical analysis of Archbishop Paul-Pierre Philippe's intervention against concelebration during Vatican II, offering scholarly insight into liturgical debates.
items.ggiConnection: Directly relates to GGI's interest in liturgical tradition and the intellectual history of the Church

items.title: St. Jerome Academy and the Call to Inclusive Catholic Schools
items.summary: Discussion of St. Jerome Academy's approach to inclusive Catholic education, examining contemporary challenges in Catholic schooling.
items.ggiConnection: Directly relevant to GGI's classical education mission and formation programs

items.title: What Can Be Done about the Universities?
items.summary: Addresses the crisis in higher education and potential solutions, highly relevant to GGI's educational mission and classical education advocacy.
items.ggiConnection: Directly connects to GGI's work in educational renewal and alternative formation models

items.title: The Need for Non-Ironic Limits: A Review of The Philosophy of Philip Rieff
items.summary: Review of Philip Rieff's philosophy examining the need for genuine cultural limits and moral boundaries in modern society. Deep intellectual analysis of contemporary cultural criticism.
items.ggiConnection: Strong alignment with GGI's cultural renewal mission and Catholic intellectual tradition themes

items.title: How To Truly KNOW Something | Dr. Trent Dougherty
items.summary: Pints with Aquinas interview with Dr. Trent Dougherty on epistemology offers deep philosophical content aligned with Catholic intellectual tradition.
items.ggiConnection: Strong alignment with GGI's faith and reason mission and philosophical formation

items.title: Why Modern Storytelling Is SO BAD | Dr. Martin Shaw
items.summary: Dr. Martin Shaw analyzes problems in modern storytelling on Pints with Aquinas, connecting narrative tradition to cultural renewal themes.
items.ggiConnection: Strong connection to GGI's cultural renewal mission and Great Books tradition

items.title: How Science Killed Materialism
items.summary: Explores how modern scientific discoveries challenge materialist worldviews, arguing that science itself points beyond purely material explanations of reality.
items.ggiConnection: Aligns with GGI's faith and reason integration, addressing fundamental questions about the relationship between science and Catholic intellectual tradition.

items.title: God and Man at MIT
items.summary: Examines the place of faith and religious life within elite academic institutions, particularly focusing on MIT's environment.
items.ggiConnection: Connects to GGI's educational mission and the integration of faith with intellectual life in higher education.

items.title: When Envy Turns Apocalyptic
items.summary: Analyzes how envy functions as a destructive force in contemporary culture, exploring its theological and social dimensions.
items.ggiConnection: Aligns with GGI's cultural analysis through Catholic moral theology and understanding of virtue and vice.

items.title: The West Distorted
items.summary: First Things analysis of how Western civilization has been misrepresented or misunderstood in contemporary discourse. Aligns well with Catholic intellectual tradition and cultural analysis mission.
items.ggiConnection: Directly relevant to GGI's cultural renewal mission and defense of Western Christian civilization

items.title: “Ecclesiacide,” Then and Now
items.summary: First Things examines historical and contemporary threats to Church institutional life, drawing parallels between past and present challenges. Substantial theological and historical analysis.
items.ggiConnection: Relevant to GGI's mission of Catholic formation and understanding institutional Catholic life

items.title: Thinking Christianly About Technology Again: Technology and the Fragmentation of Society
items.summary: Notre Dame publication examines how technology contributes to social fragmentation through a Christian anthropological lens. Thoughtful analysis of contemporary cultural challenges.
items.ggiConnection: Connects to GGI's cultural renewal mission and concern for community formation in the digital age

items.title: What to do with the conversion boom?
items.summary: Analysis of increasing conversion rates to Catholicism and pastoral challenges this presents. Addresses formation, community integration, and cultural renewal themes.
items.ggiConnection: Strongly connects to GGI mission of formation and cultural renewal through Catholic community building
